chapter_title.en: The Call to Prayer and the Establishment of Prayer and the Reward of the Muezzins

text:
913 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَانَ اِسْمُ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يُكَرَّرُ فِي اَلْأَذَانِ فَأَوَّلُ مَنْ حَذَفَهُ اِبْنُ أَرْوَى.
وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّهُ كَانَ بِالْمَدِينَةِ إِذَا أَذَّنَ اَلْمُؤَذِّنُ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ نَادَى مُنَادٍ حُرِّمَ اَلْبَيْعُ لِقَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «يٰا أَيُّهَا اَلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذٰا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلاٰةِ مِنْ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فَاسْعَوْا إِلىٰ ذِكْرِ اَللّٰهِ وَ ذَرُوا اَلْبَيْعَ» »

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.913 - Imam (as) said:
"The name of the Prophet (sw) used to be repeated in the adhan (call to prayer), and the first one to remove it was Ibn Arwa."
It is also reported: "In Medina, when the mu'adhdhin (caller to prayer) would make the adhan on Jumu’ah, a caller would announce that trading was forbidden due to the statement of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
'O’ you who believe, when the call is made for prayer on the day of Jumu’ah, hasten to the remembrance of Allah (swt) and leave off trade.'" (Surah Al-Jumu'ah 62:9)
